data: Baldassare Romano, Antichità termitane (Palermo: Tipografia di Francesco Lao, 1838)
Funerary epitaph for Marcus Hortesius Primus
Three lines of Latin text, with triangular interpuncts

Letter shapes and orientation are slightly erratic, but the letters are extremely thin and with exaggerated serif tails, with a strong leftward slant to the vertical axis. The long legs of the "R"s are particularly distinctive.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2023-07-06.
Crispi, 1824, Spiegazione di due iscrizioni trovate nella citta di Termini
funerary epitaph for marcus hortesius primus

2Primus
3vix(it) · an(nis) · [---]
Two lines of Latin letters, with visible guidelines, with traces of a third line below.
Deep v-cut Latin letters, with a reversed 'N', pronounced terminal serifs.

1Ex d(ecreto) d(ecurionum)
2in fro(nte) p(edes) XXV
3in agro p(edes) XVI
Four lines of Latin letters, line 1 set far left, lines 2-4 indented with a consistent margin. Only 8mm is preserved below line 4, leaving it uncertain whether the text continued below or not.
Neat v-cut letters of moderately wide module. P is open, O large, M with near vertical first and last strokes, full-length middle strokes.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2023-07-07

1Antonius Iulianus
2v(ixit) a(nnis) XXV
Three lines of Latin letters, decreasing in size, and probably centred on the stone.
Letters are somewhat irregular with some serifs and the M regularly extended above the apex. Multi-pointed interpuncts between words.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2018-07-10

1[---]Ṃ · M͡argar
2[v(ixit)] ạ(nnis) · IIII · m(ensibus) · XI
3d(iebus) · VII
Fragmentary Latin inscription recording two individuals

Two lines of Latin text. Comma shaped interpunct in line 1, with what appears to be a long downward linear interpunct in line 2. Heavily abraded on sides and bottom. Missing text on both left and right.
Letters are tall, and serifs are used. Spaces between the letters are uneven.
46 (measurement of only complete letter, other letters survive only to a maximum of 40mm)
fragmentary latin inscription recording two individuals

2ḷ(ocus) · p(ublice) · d(atus) · in · fr(onte) · p(edes) · XXV
3in · agr(o) [p(edes) ---]
Four complete lines of Latin text approximately centred
Text is shallowly incised, irregular and rough. Letters are not straight, nor in line with each other. Very basic serifs, on occasional letters. Long tail on "L" in line 4.
Ferrua dates to the fourth century on the basis of the lettering.
Castelli, 1769, Siciliae et objacentium insularum veterum inscriptionum nova collectio

Funerary plot for Marcus Arruntius Brocchus
Five lines of Latin letters, seemingly centred on the stone.
Rather worn, partially v-cut letters with simple terminal serifs and triangular comma-shaped interpuncts.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J.Prag 05.07.2023

Funerary inscription for M. Antonius Natalis
Three lines of Latin text, roughly centred, in top 1/3 of cippus. Abrasions on surface near text. Large, roughly triangular interpuncts.
Wide and roughly carved letters, although the letters are nonetheless well formed. Traces of serifs.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J.Prag 04.07.23
funerary inscription for m. antonius natalis

1M(arcus) · Antonius
2Natalis · vix(it)
3an(nis) · XII

Three lines of Latin text, line 3 is almost entirely missing. Ill-defined interpuncts used, some triangular. Large vacat at the end of line 3. Abrasions at the beginning of line 2 frustrate precision of comprehension.
Slightly haphazard letters, but otherwise tall and well-spaced. Serifs used on most letters.

1L(ucius) · Herennius
2C̣ẹrtus · v(ixit) · a(nnis) · XIII
3have
It seems highly likely that the text was centered over two lines, and that elements are missing from the right.
Large simple letters, lacking serifs and with a broad module.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; image from ILTermini

1Lucia · (mulieris) · l(iberta) ·
2hic · sita
Remains of three lines of Latin letters, of which the first much larger than the second and third, with indications that the text was centred on the stone.
Fairly tall narrow letters, deeply incised, L in line 1 with long descending lower stroke; O in line three smaller and mid-line; substantial but crude interpuncts.
